title: Kailarsenia campanula
taxonomy: Kailarsenia campanula is classified within the family Boraginaceae, order Borales, and class Magnoliopsida. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and is a flowering plant species. Its classification as a species within the genus Kailarsenia places it in the family Boraginaceae. The species is native to Southeast Asia and is also known as the Southeast Asian species within the Boraginaceae family. The classification of Kailarsenia campanula places it in the larger clade of flowering plants.

intro: Kailarsenia campanula is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Lamiaceae, commonly found in the Western Ghats of India. Traditionally, the plant has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, sore throat, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of K. campanula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and ointments for skin and wound care.
